data_understanding.modules¶

data_understanding.modules.analysis¶

Electric Truck Data Analysis Module.

Provides analysis and visualization utilities for understanding electric truck trip datasets, including trip durations, distances, payloads, geographic locations, and vehicle group distributions.

Example

>>> analyzer = TruckDataAnalyzer(cfg)
>>> analyzer.run()
class data_understanding.modules.analysis.TruckDataAnalyzer(cfg: DictConfig)[source]¶

Bases: object

Analyzes electric truck trip datasets for data understanding purposes.

Loads verified parquet trip files and computes statistics and visualizations covering trip locations, durations, distances, payloads, and vehicle group distributions. Results are saved as JSON summaries and publication-quality PDF/PNG plots.

cfg¶

Hydra configuration object. Must contain a data_understanding sub-config with the fields file_extension, verified_data_dir, and results_dir.

Type:

DictConfig

verified_data_dir¶

Directory containing verified parquet trip files.

Type:

Path

file_extension¶

File suffix used when globbing trip files (e.g. '.parquet').

Type:

str

results_dir¶

Directory where JSON summaries and plots are saved.

Type:

Path

collect_trip_metadata() → dict[str, int | float | list[float]][source]¶

Collects and saves descriptive statistics for trip metadata.

Iterates over all verified parquet files and aggregates statistics for payload, distance, and duration. Results are saved to results_dir/meta_data.json.

Returns:

Metadata dictionary containing total_trips, min/max/mean/std and sequence values for payload, distance, and duration.

Return type:

dict[str, int | float | list[float]]

generate_trips_summary() → dict[str, list[str] | dict[str, any]][source]¶

Generates a statistical summary of trips grouped by country.

Reads trips_by_country.json and computes per-country trip counts, percentages, and multi-country breakdowns. Results are saved to results_dir/trips_summary.json.

Returns:

Summary dictionary containing country lists, per-key trip

counts and percentages, and a multi_country_breakdown entry.

Return type:

dict[str, list[str] | dict[str, any]]

Note

This function was developed with the assistance of Claude AI (Anthropic).

get_datasets() → tuple[list[Path], list[str]][source]¶

Gets all trip files in the verified data directory.

Returns:

A tuple of (paths, filenames) where paths is a sorted list of pathlib.Path objects matching file_extension, and filenames contains the corresponding stems (no extension).

Return type:

tuple[list[Path], list[str]]

get_trip_durations_and_distances() → DataFrame[source]¶

Computes average trip durations and distances grouped by vehicle group.

Reads all verified parquet files, computes per-trip durations and distances, and aggregates them by vehicle group (v_group).

Returns:

Summary DataFrame with columns v_group, n_trips, avg_duration (HH:MM:SS), and avg_distance_km.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

get_trip_locations() → dict[str, list[str]][source]¶

Assigns country and region labels to all GPS points with reverse geocoding.

Loads all verified parquet files, reverse-geocodes every GPS coordinate, and groups trip filenames by their dominant country or country combination. Results are saved to results_dir/trips_by_country.json.

Returns:

Mapping from country key (e.g. 'DE', 'DE,FR') to a list of trip filenames belonging to that key.

Return type:

dict[str, list[str]]

Note

This function was developed with the assistance of Claude AI (Anthropic).

plot_trip_metadata() → None[source]¶

Plots and saves a boxplot of trip distance, duration, and payload.

Reads meta_data.json and produces a side-by-side boxplot for distance (km), duration (min), and payload (t). Saves both PDF and PNG to results_dir.

plot_trips_summary() → None[source]¶

Plots and saves a vertical bar chart of trip distribution by country.

Reads trips_by_country.json, separates single-country and multi-country trips, and produces a log-scale bar chart with count and percentage annotations. Saves both as PDF and PNG files to results_dir.

plot_v_groups() → None[source]¶

Plots and saves a vertical bar chart of trip counts per vehicle group.

Reads all verified parquet files, counts trips per vehicle group (v_group), and produces a log-scale bar chart with count and percentage annotations. Saves both as PDF and PNG files to results_dir.

run() → None[source]¶

Runs the full data understanding pipeline in sequence.

Executes all analysis and visualization steps:

  1. Assigns country labels to trip GPS points

  2. Generates a country-level trip summary

  3. Plots trip distribution by country

  4. Plots trip counts by vehicle group

  5. Collects trip metadata statistics

  6. Plots trip metadata boxplots

static seconds_to_hms(seconds: float) → str[source]¶

Converts a duration in seconds to an HH:MM:SS string.

Parameters:

seconds (float) – Duration in seconds.

Returns:

Formatted duration string in HH:MM:SS format.

Return type:

str

Note

This function was developed with the assistance of ChatGPT.

setup_dirs() → None[source]¶

Creates required output directories if they do not already exist.

data_understanding.modules.constants¶

Constants for electric truck data understanding.

This module defines vehicle identifiers, vehicle groups, vehicle-to-group mappings, and signal column mappings used throughout the data understanding pipeline.

data_understanding.modules.constants.VEHICLE_IDS¶

Supported vehicle identifiers.

Type:

list[str]

data_understanding.modules.constants.VEHICLE_GROUPS¶

Mapping from group index to member vehicle IDs.

Type:

dict[int, list[str]]

data_understanding.modules.constants.VEHICLE_ID_TO_GROUP¶

Mapping from vehicle ID to its group index.

Type:

dict[str, int]

data_understanding.modules.constants.SIGNAL_MAPPING¶

Expected signal names and their column indices used for schema validation and ordering.

Type:

dict[str, int]

data_understanding.modules.quality_verification¶

Electric Truck Data Quality Verification Module.

This module validates processed electric truck trip datasets before they are used in the data preparation stage. It performs structural checks, feature validation, temporal consistency verification, and trajectory visualization.

Example

>>> verificator = TruckDataQualityVerificator(cfg)
>>> verificator.run()
class data_understanding.modules.quality_verification.TruckDataQualityVerificator(cfg: DictConfig)[source]¶

Bases: object

Performs quality verification for electric truck trip datasets.

Loads processed trip files, validates schema consistency, checks temporal sampling constraints, and generates verification reports and trajectory visualizations.

cfg¶

Hydra configuration object. Must contain a data_understanding sub-config with the fields file_extension, processed_data_dir, verified_data_dir, and results_dir.

Type:

DictConfig

file_extension¶

File suffix used when globbing trip files (e.g. '.parquet').

Type:

str

vehicle_id_to_group¶

Mapping from vehicle identifier to vehicle group.

Type:

dict[str, int]

vehicle_ids¶

Supported vehicle identifiers.

Type:

list[str]

signal_mapping¶

Expected signals and their column ordering.

Type:

dict[str, int]

processed_data_dir¶

Directory containing processed datasets.

Type:

Path

verified_data_dir¶

Directory where verified parquet files are written.

Type:

Path

results_dir¶

Directory where verification reports are saved.

Type:

Path

plots_dir¶

Directory where trajectory HTML plots are saved.

Type:

Path

add_vehicle_id_and_group(df: DataFrame, filename: str) → DataFrame[source]¶

Extracts and assigns vehicle ID and group from the trip filename.

Parses the filename to identify the vehicle identifier and looks up the corresponding vehicle group from vehicle_id_to_group.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Trip DataFrame to annotate.

  • filename (str) – Stem of the source file containing the vehicle ID.

Returns:

Input DataFrame with added v_id and v_group columns.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

Raises:

  • ValueError – If no valid vehicle ID is found in the filename.

  • ValueError – If multiple vehicle IDs are found in the filename.

check_format_and_missing_data(df: DataFrame, filename: str) → DataFrame[source]¶

Checks for missing values and validates 1 Hz temporal sampling.

Verifies that all signal timestamps are spaced exactly 1 second apart and that no NaN values are present. Returns a single-row report DataFrame summarising the findings.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Trip DataFrame to validate. Must contain a

  • datetime. (signal_time column parseable as)

  • filename (str) – Stem of the source file used as the report identifier.

Returns:

Single-row report with columns filename, valid,

has_missing_values, is_1hz, and wrong_index_groups.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

get_datasets() → tuple[list[Path], list[str]][source]¶

Gets all trip files in the verified data directory.

Returns:

A tuple of (paths, filenames) where paths is a sorted list of pathlib.Path objects matching file_extension, and filenames contains the corresponding stems (no extension).

Return type:

tuple[list[Path], list[str]]

plot_trip_trajectory(df: DataFrame, filename: str) → None[source]¶

Renders an interactive Folium map with trip trajectory and key statistics.

Draws the full GPS trajectory as a black polyline with start and end markers. A dashboard overlay displays vehicle ID, group, distance, average speed, SoC, and mean battery temperature. The map is saved to plots_dir.

Parameters:

  • df (pd.DataFrame) – Verified trip DataFrame. Must contain the following columns: latitude_cval_ippc, longitude_cval_ippc, v_id, v_group, hirestotalvehdist_cval_icuc, vehspd_cval_cpc, hv_bat_soc_cval_bms1, hv_batavcelltemp_cval_bms1. A detailed description of these CAN signals can be found in utils/can_signals.json.

  • filename (str) – Stem of the source file. Used as the map title and output filename.

Note

This function was developed with the assistance of Claude AI (Anthropic).

run() → None[source]¶

Runs the full quality verification pipeline.

For each trip file, the pipeline:

  1. Assigns vehicle ID and group from the filename

  2. Validates expected signal columns and ordering

  3. Checks for missing values and 1 Hz sampling

  4. Saves the verified parquet file to verified_data_dir

  5. Generates a trajectory HTML plot in plots_dir

After processing all files, failed verification reports are saved to results_dir/failed_quality_verification.json.

setup_dirs() → None[source]¶

Creates required output directories if they do not already exist.

verify_expected_features(df: DataFrame) → DataFrame[source]¶

Validates that the DataFrame contains exactly the expected signal columns.

Checks for missing and unexpected columns against signal_mapping, then reorders columns to match the expected ordering.

Parameters:

df (pd.DataFrame) – Trip DataFrame to validate.

Returns:

Input DataFrame with columns reordered to match signal_mapping.

Return type:

pd.DataFrame

Raises:

  • ValueError – If any expected signals are missing from the DataFrame.

  • ValueError – If any unexpected signals are present in the DataFrame.
